reasoning: The resolution criteria explicitly depend on the value of customs duties collected in FY 2025. In FY 2024, the U.S. collected $82.2 billion in customs duties. According to the CBO's July 2025 Monthly Budget Review, customs duties had increased by $70 billion (or 112 percent) relative to the same period in the prior year, driven by tariffs implemented since February 2025. This puts the 10-month YTD customs duties at approximately $132.5 billion. Even with high continuing tariff collections in the final two months of FY 2025, total customs duties for the fiscal year will land somewhere around $160-180 billion. This falls drastically short of the $500 billion minimum required by the bracket criteria of this question. Therefore, it is virtually impossible for customs duties to reach the $500 billion to $1 trillion range.
